author: Concomitant
description: Fetches any Wikipedia article and drops it into a new or existing Note in your Vault. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Concomitant
manifest.description: Import a Wikipedia page as clean Markdown with [[wikilinks]], LaTeX math, images, and plaintext references — stripping navboxes and template fluff.
manifest.name: Wikipedia Importer

name: Wikipedia Importer

releases.author: AedraAelynn
releases.description:
## [1.1.2] ### Fixed - **SVG images no longer look blurry.** Wikipedia serves every image — including vector diagrams — as a pre-rendered raster thumbnail. Vectors (`.svg`) and animations (`.gif`) now resolve to their true original file instead, so diagrams stay crisp and gifs actually animate. - **A box header spanning several lines (e.g. a native-name box giving a term's transliteration, translation, and script) could break out of its bold label** instead of collapsing onto one line — the raw `
` line breaks were left inside the `**...**` span. Multi-line box headers are now flattened to a single "; "-joined line, matching how infobox fields already handle multi-value cells. ### Added - **Infoboxes are imported again**, as a collapsed "Quick facts" callout of label/value pairs instead of being stripped entirely. Section dividers (e.g. "Personal details") are kept as bold group labels, and multi-value fields (e.g. a list of languages) are joined onto one line instead of running together. **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/AedraAelynn/Wikipedia-Importer/compare/1.1.1...1.1.2

releases.author: AedraAelynn
releases.description:
# 🔗 Wikipedia Importer `1.1.0` is out!

**Turn any Wikipedia page into a clean, fully-linked Obsidian note — in one command.**

---

## New Features Added:

**🏷️ Create `tag` entries for new imports** (Suggestion by: [VChristinne](https://github.com/VChristinne))
Any `Note` can now have `tag` entries that are automatically imported directly from Wikipedia's own categories the moment they land, so your imports organize themselves; you can, of course, create your own `tag` entries manually. You can put these `tag` entries in YAML frontmatter, inline `#tags`, or both. Add your own `Fixed tags`, get prompted for `tag` entries per-import, or switch `Automatic tagging` off entirely.

**🔗 In-text citations that actually link**
Reference markers now appear in the body as footnotes (`[^1]`) that jump straight to the source at the bottom — exactly like Wikipedia. Where a citation has a URL, the footnote carries an external `[source]` link too.

**⚙️ Link & image control**
Choose how internal links are written — `[[wikilinks]]` (build your graph), Markdown links (point to Wikipedia online, a `Note` will not be generated), or no links at all for full customization. Same for images: embed inline, link only, or skip them altogether.

---

**Also new:** "Further reading" sections are preserved · mis-cased page titles now resolve via search · the `# Title` link and `source:` property are both toggleable · explanatory and imported `Notes` are separated from citations.

**Fixed:** maintenance banners ("This article needs additional citations") and fundraising notices are stripped · banner icons, license badges, and rendered equations no longer get grabbed as the lead image of a `Note`. · `v · t · e` navigation controls no longer leak in as `[[Template talk:…]]` links · Wikipedia's stylesheet CSS no longer bleeds into citation text · relative-project links (Wiktionary, Commons, etc.) removed. Formatting fixes.

---

Feedback and bug reports very welcome — thanks for your support!!!

releases.author: AedraAelynn
releases.description:
Wikipedia Importer fetches a Wikipedia article and drops it into a new or existing `Note` in your vault as tidy Markdown: every Wikipedia link becomes an internal `[[wikilink]]`, equations become Obsidian-rendered $\LaTeX$ (WIP), images and animated gifs come through, and most of the clutter is stripped away. What you're left with is a readable, navigable `Note` that plugs straight into your knowledge graph and `Vault`.

Changes:
- Fixed `manifest.json` metadata and versioning.
- Added additional details in `README.md` for manual installation.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/AedraAelynn/Wikipedia-Importer/compare/1.0.0...1.0.2
